body:
### Problem

### Context

`vim.secure.read()`'s view path used to open a scratch buffer and explicitly `set modifiable=false` in #20956. In #21283, that was replaced with `:sview` so the viewed buffer would have a real file name and :trust could operate on the current buffer. The review discussion there was about trusting what the user actually sees in the buffer
and about fixing :trust for viewed files, not about intentionally allowing edits before trust. 

Given the context and lack of commentary on the latter pr, since `:sview` only sets `'readonly'` and leaves the normal default of `'modifiable'` alone, the current behavior seems to be a consequence of the refactor rather than an intentional decision (at least, I couldn't find a reason).

### Motivation

1. generally seems reasonable to force people into a "view, trust, then edit" flow rather than "view, maybe edit, then trust with edits on top"
2. (appealing to authority) this behavior is mirrored in other editors/environments generally (i.e. VSCode blocks you from opening folders *before* you can edit things)
3. selfishly, doing this would unambiguously resolves #39017, since secure-read empty buffers would *stay* empty by blocking modification

### Expected behavior

Mark secure-read buffers `nomodifiable`.
title: fix(secure): make secure view buffers nomodifiable

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
